3. Configure nginx as a reverse proxy

For Windows 3.11, I should use:
- qemu-system-i386 with appropriate CPU/RAM settings
- Snapshot mode (-snapshot)
- VNC on :1 (port 5901)
- Monitor socket at /tmp/qemu-monitor.sock
- The image is a raw disk, so I'll use it as an IDE hard

drive

thinking.text:
The disk image is a raw DOS/MBR boot sector image with a FAT16 partition (ID=0x6). This is a bootable Windows 3.11 disk image. Now I need to:

1. Launch QEMU with the proper settings
2. Set up noVNC/websockify for web access on port 80
3. Configure nginx as a reverse proxy

For Windows 3.11, I should use:
- qemu-system-i386 with appropriate CPU/RAM settings
- Snapshot mode (-snapshot)
- VNC on :1 (port 5901)
- Monitor socket at /tmp/qemu-monitor.sock
- The image is a raw disk, so I'll use it as an IDE hard drive

Let me start QEMU in the background, then set up the VNC web interface.
